question: I have chest pain and shortness of breath
Response: You should consult a Cardiologist (SpJP) immediately. Chest pain with shortness of breath may indicate a cardiac condition. Seek emergency care if pain is severe or radiates to the arm or jaw.

question: My chest feels tight and I feel faint
Response: These symptoms may be cardiac in origin. Please visit a Cardiologist (SpJP) as soon as possible. Do not ignore sudden faintness combined with chest tightness.

question: I have persistent headache that won't go away
Response: A Neurologist (SpS) can help evaluate persistent headaches. If the headache is sudden and severe or accompanied by vision changes or weakness

question: I have been having trouble speaking and walking
Response: These are potential stroke warning signs. Go to an emergency room immediately. A Neurologist (SpS) will assess and manage this condition.

question: I fractured my bone in an accident
Response: Please see an Orthopedic Surgeon (SpOT) for fracture management. X-rays and proper immobilization are essential for recovery.

question: My back is hurting a lot and I feel weak
Response: Back pain with general weakness may need evaluation by an Internist (SpPD) or Orthopedic Surgeon (SpOT) depending on the cause. An Internist can first assess whether it is systemic or structural.

question: I have a skin rash and skin infection
Response: A Dermatologist (SpKK) specializes in skin conditions including infections
column4: and lesions. Please schedule an appointment for proper diagnosis and treatment.

question: My vision is blurry and I have eye problems
Response: Please consult an Ophthalmologist (SpM) for any vision-related issues. Early assessment can prevent further deterioration.

question: I have been having high fever and diarrhea
Response: Fever with diarrhea may indicate a gastrointestinal infection. An Internist (SpPD) can assess and manage these symptoms. Stay hydrated while awaiting your appointment.

question: I feel nauseous and I keep vomiting
Response: Persistent vomiting can have many causes. An Internist (SpPD) or Gastroenterologist can help. If vomiting is accompanied by blood or severe pain

question: I have a stomachache and gastric problems
Response: Stomach pain and gastric issues are commonly managed by an Internist (SpPD). A gastroscopy may be needed to diagnose underlying conditions like gastric ulcers.

question: I have shortness of breath and cough
Response: Respiratory symptoms such as shortness of breath and cough should be evaluated by a Pulmonologist (Paru). Conditions like asthma
column4: or pneumonia may be the cause.

question: I have chronic cough and breathing difficulty
Response: A Pulmonologist (Paru) specializes in respiratory diseases. Chronic cough with breathing difficulty may indicate COPD
column4: or other lung conditions requiring proper diagnosis.

question: I cut myself and the wound is not healing
Response: A General Surgeon (SpB) can help with wound management. If the cut is deep or infected
column3: please seek medical care promptly to avoid complications.

question: I have something stuck in my throat
Response: A foreign object stuck in the throat is an ENT (THT) emergency. Please go to an ENT specialist or emergency room immediately.

question: My ear hurts and I have difficulty hearing
Response: Ear pain and hearing problems are managed by an ENT specialist (THT). Conditions such as ear infection or fluid buildup may be the cause.

question: I have sore throat and nasal congestion
Response: These are common ENT conditions. An ENT Specialist (THT) can assess whether the cause is infection

question: I have been diagnosed with hypertension
Response: Hypertension is commonly managed by an Internist (SpPD) or Cardiologist (SpJP) depending on severity. Regular monitoring and medication compliance are important.

question: I am a diabetic patient and need medical advice
Response: Diabetes management is handled by an Internist (SpPD). Regular monitoring of blood glucose
column4: and organ function is essential for long-term health.

question: I have liver disease and need a specialist
Response: Liver diseases are managed by an Internist (SpPD) with hepatology expertise. Conditions such as hepatitis
column4: or fatty liver need proper workup and management.

question: I had a stroke before and want a follow up
Response: Stroke survivors should follow up with a Neurologist (SpS). Rehabilitation and secondary prevention are key to reducing recurrence risk.

question: I have been diagnosed with a tumor
Response: Tumor management requires an Internist (SpPD) or Oncologist depending on the type and stage. Please consult your referring doctor for specialist referral.

question: I have respiratory disease and breathing problems
Response: Respiratory disease is managed by a Pulmonologist (Paru). Proper lung function tests and imaging will help determine the best treatment plan.

question: I have heart disease and chest discomfort
Response: Heart disease with chest discomfort requires evaluation by a Cardiologist (SpJP). Do not delay consultation if symptoms are worsening.

question: Which doctor should I see for a broken arm
Response: For a broken arm
column3: please see an Orthopedic Surgeon (SpOT). They will perform imaging and decide on casting or surgical intervention.

question: Who treats skin infections
Response: Skin infections are treated by a Dermatologist (SpKK). Bacterial
column4: and viral skin infections each require different treatment approaches.

question: What specialist handles eye problems
Response: Eye problems are handled by an Ophthalmologist (SpM). Regular eye checkups are recommended especially for diabetic and hypertensive patients.

question: Which specialist treats lung disease
Response: Lung and respiratory diseases are treated by a Pulmonologist (Paru). They manage conditions such as asthma

question: What doctor handles stomach problems
Response: Stomach and digestive problems are managed by an Internist (SpPD) or Gastroenterologist. Symptoms like pain
column4: and acid reflux are common presenting complaints.

question: Who should I see for ear and nose problems
Response: Ear
column4: and throat problems are handled by an ENT Specialist (THT). These include ear infections

question: I have cold sweats and feel faint often
Response: Cold sweats with fainting episodes may indicate a cardiovascular or neurological problem. Please see an Internist (SpPD) for initial workup to determine the right specialist.

question: I feel very weak and tired all the time
Response: Persistent weakness and fatigue can have many causes including anemia
column4: or chronic infection. An Internist (SpPD) is the best starting point for evaluation.

question: I have trouble seeing at night
Response: Night vision problems should be assessed by an Ophthalmologist (SpM). It may indicate retinal changes

question: My joints are swollen and painful
Response: Joint pain and swelling may indicate arthritis or other rheumatic diseases. An Internist (SpPD) with rheumatology focus or an Orthopedic Surgeon (SpOT) can help.

question: I have a mole that is changing color
Response: A changing mole may be a sign of skin cancer. Please consult a Dermatologist (SpKK) immediately for evaluation and possible biopsy.

question: I keep getting headaches when I wake up
Response: Morning headaches can be related to sleep apnea
column4: or neurological causes. A Neurologist (SpS) can conduct appropriate evaluations.

question: Who can help with dizziness and fainting
Response: Dizziness and fainting can be caused by cardiovascular
column4: or inner ear issues. An Internist (SpPD) can do initial workup and refer to the appropriate specialist.

question: I have been having back pain for months
Response: Chronic back pain requires evaluation by an Orthopedic Surgeon (SpOT) or a Neurologist (SpS) if nerve involvement is suspected. Imaging may be needed.

question: What is SpJP
Response: SpJP stands for Spesialis Jantung dan Pembuluh Darah
column3: meaning Cardiologist. They specialize in heart and vascular diseases.

question: What is SpPD
Response: SpPD stands for Spesialis Penyakit Dalam
column3: meaning Internist. They manage a wide range of internal medicine conditions including metabolic and infectious diseases.

question: What is SpS
Response: SpS stands for Spesialis Saraf
column3: meaning Neurologist. They treat diseases of the brain

question: What is SpOT
Response: SpOT stands for Spesialis Ortopedi dan Traumatologi
column3: meaning Orthopedic Surgeon. They handle bone

question: What is THT
Response: THT stands for Telinga Hidung Tenggorokan
column3: meaning ENT Specialist. They manage diseases of the ear

question: What is SpKK
Response: SpKK stands for Spesialis Kulit dan Kelamin

question: What is SpM
Response: SpM stands for Spesialis Mata
column3: meaning Ophthalmologist. They diagnose and treat all eye conditions.

question: What is Paru
Response: Paru refers to Spesialis Paru
column3: meaning Pulmonologist. They manage lung and respiratory tract diseases.

question: What is SpB
Response: SpB stands for Spesialis Bedah
column3: meaning General Surgeon. They perform surgical procedures for a variety of conditions.
